methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials, a Series D climate and energy company founded by J.B. Straubel, has raised $1.125B+ and carries a post-money valuation of $6.0B+. The valuation benchmark set includes three same-sector, same-stage private peers (KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M), plus a broader comp universe spanning 8 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $6.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, implies a forward valuation of $9.0B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. The peer-median result appears attenuated relative to Redwood's funding profile and stage advancement, likely reflecting both the earlier stage of the peer sample and heterogeneity in capital deployment across climate-tech subsectors.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.0B. The $5.0B midpoint sits between the peer-median floor and the stage-progression uplift, and aligns closely with the stated $6.0B+ current valuation. Key uncertainties include capital intensity and deployment timing in battery recycling operations, and differentiation relative to an increasingly crowded climate-tech funding landscape.
